Fighter                                                                                Creating a Fighter
A human in clanging plate armor holds her shield before                                As you build your fighter, think about two related ele-
her as she runs toward the massed goblins. An elf behind                               ments of your character’s background: Where did you get
her, clad in studded leather armor, peppers the goblins                                your combat training, and what set you apart from the
with arrows loosed from his exquisite bow. The half-orc                                mundane warriors around you? Were you particularly
nearby shouts orders, helping the two combatants coordi-                               ruthless? Did you get extra help from a mentor, perhaps
nate their assault to the best advantage.                                              because of your exceptional dedication? What drove you
  A dwarf in chain mail interposes his shield between the                              to this training in the first place? A threat to your home-
ogre’s club and his companion, knocking the deadly blow                                land, a thirst for revenge, or a need to prove yourself
aside. His companion, a half-elf in scale armor, swings                                might all have been factors.
two scimitars in a blinding whirl as she circles the ogre,                               You might have enjoyed formal training in a noble’s
looking for a blind spot in its defenses.                                              army or in a local militia. Perhaps you trained in a war
  A gladiator fights for sport in an arena, a master with                              academy, learning strategy, tactics, and military history.
his trident and net, skilled at toppling foes and moving                               Or you might be self-taught—unpolished but well tested.
them around for the crowd’s delight—and his own tactical                               Did you take up the sword as a way to escape the limits
advantage. His opponent’s sword flares with blue light                                 of life on a farm, or are you following a proud family tra-
an instant before she sends lightning flashing forth to                                dition? Where did you acquire your weapons and armor?
smite him.                                                                             They might have been military issue or family heirlooms,
  All of these heroes are fighters, perhaps the most di-                               or perhaps you scrimped and saved for years to buy them.
verse class of characters in the worlds of Dungeons &                                  Your armaments are now among your most important
Dragons. Questing knights, conquering overlords, royal                                 possessions—the only things that stand between you and
champions, elite foot soldiers, hardened mercenaries,                                  death’s embrace.
and bandit kings—as fighters, they all share an unparal-
leled mastery with weapons and armor, and a thorough
                                                                                       Quick Build
knowledge of the skills of combat. And they are well                                   You can make a fighter quickly by following these sug-
acquainted with death, both meting it out and staring it                               gestions. First, make Strength or Dexterity your highest
defiantly in the face.                                                                 ability score, depending on whether you want to focus
                                                                                       on melee weapons or on archery (or finesse weapons).
Well-Rounded Specialists                                                               Your next-highest score should be Constitution. Second,
                                                                                       choose the soldier background.
Fighters learn the basics of all combat styles. Every
fighter can swing an axe, fence with a rapier, wield a
longsword or a greatsword, use a bow, and even trap foes
                                                                                       The Fighter
in a net with some degree of skill. Likewise, a fighter                                            Proficiency
is adept with shields and every form of armor. Beyond                                    Level       Bonus           Features
that basic degree of familiarity, each fighter specializes                                1st          +2            Fighting Style, Second Wind
in a certain style of combat. Some concentrate on ar-                                     2nd          +2            Action Surge (one use)
chery, some on fighting with two weapons at once, and                                     3rd          +2            Martial Archetype
some on augmenting their martial skills with magic.                                       4th          +2            Ability Score Improvement
This combination of broad general ability and exten-                                      5th          +3            Extra Attack
sive specialization makes fighters superior combatants                                    6th          +3            Ability Score Improvement
on battlefields and in dungeons alike.
                                                                                          7th          +3            Martial Archetype feature
Trained for Danger                                                                        8th          +3            Ability Score Improvement
                                                                                          9th          +4            Indomitable (one use)
Not every member of the city watch, the village militia,                                 10th          +4            Martial Archetype feature
or the queen’s army is a fighter. Most of these troops are
                                                                                         11th          +4            Extra Attack (2)
relatively untrained soldiers with only the most basic
                                                                                         12th          +4            Ability Score Improvement
combat knowledge. Veteran soldiers, military officers,
trained bodyguards, dedicated knights, and similar fig-                                  13th          +5            Indomitable (two uses)
ures are fighters.                                                                       14th          +5            Ability Score Improvement
  Some fighters feel drawn to use their training as adven-                               15th          +5            Martial Archetype feature
turers. The dungeon delving, monster slaying, and other                                  16th          +5            Ability Score Improvement
dangerous work common among adventurers is second                                        17th          +6            Action Surge (two uses), Indomitable
nature for a fighter, not all that different from the life he                                                        (three uses)
or she left behind. There are greater risks, perhaps, but                                18th            +6          Martial Archetype feature
also much greater rewards—few fighters in the city watch                                 19th            +6          Ability Score Improvement
have the opportunity to discover a magic flame tongue                                    20th            +6          Extra Attack (3)
sword, for example.

Defense
                                                                                       Extra Attack
While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.                                Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of
                                                                                       once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
Dueling                                                                                  The number of attacks increases to three when you
When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and                                   reach 11th level in this class and to four when you reach
no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls                                  20th level in this class.
with that weapon.
                                                                                       Indomitable
Great Weapon Fighting                                                                  Beginning at 9th level, you can reroll a saving throw that
When you roll a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack you                               you fail. If you do so, you must use the new roll, and you
make with a melee weapon that you are wielding with                                    can’t use this feature again until you finish a long rest.
two hands, you can reroll the die and must use the new                                   You can use this feature twice between long rests
roll, even if the new roll is a 1 or a 2. The weapon must                              starting at 13th level and three times between long rests
have the two-handed or versatile property for you to gain                              starting at 17th level.
this benefit.

Protection
When a creature you can see attacks a target other than
you that is within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction
to impose disadvantage on the attack roll. You must be
wielding a shield.

                   ing their fighting prowess. The martial archetype you                                  forward through the dungeon hall. She presses an ear
                   choose to emulate reflects your approach.                                              to the door, then pulls out a set of tools and picks the
                                                                                                          lock in the blink of an eye. Then she disappears into the
                   Champion                                                                               shadows as her fighter friend moves forward to kick the
                   The archetypal Champion focuses on the development                                     door open.
                   of raw physical power honed to deadly perfection.                                         A human lurks in the shadows of an alley while his
                   Those who model themselves on this archetype combine                                   accomplice prepares for her part in the ambush. When
                   rigorous training with physical excellence to deal devas-                              their target—a notorious slaver—passes the alleyway,
                   tating blows.                                                                          the accomplice cries out, the slaver comes to investigate,
                                                                                                          and the assassin’s blade cuts his throat before he can
                   Improved Critical                                                                      make a sound.
                   Beginning when you choose this archetype at 3rd                                           Suppressing a giggle, a gnome waggles her fingers and
                   level, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll                              magically lifts the key ring from the guard’s belt. In a mo-
                   of 19 or 20.                                                                           ment, the keys are in her hand, the cell door is open, and
                                                                                                          she and her companions are free to make their escape.
                   Remarkable Athlete                                                                        Rogues rely on skill, stealth, and their foes’ vulnerabil-
                   Starting at 7th level, you can add half your proficiency                               ities to get the upper hand in any situation. They have a
                   bonus (round up) to any Strength, Dexterity, or Con-                                   knack for finding the solution to just about any problem,
                   stitution check you make that doesn’t already use your                                 demonstrating a resourcefulness and versatility that is
                   proficiency bonus.                                                                     the cornerstone of any successful adventuring party.
                     In addition, when you make a running long jump, the
                   distance you can cover increases by a number of feet                                   Skill and Precision
                   equal to your Strength modifier.                                                       Rogues devote as much effort to mastering the use of
                   Additional Fighting Style                                                              a variety of skills as they do to perfecting their combat
                                                                                                          abilities, giving them a broad expertise that few other
                   At 10th level, you can choose a second option from the
                                                                                                          characters can match. Many rogues focus on stealth and
                   Fighting Style class feature.
                                                                                                          deception, while others refine the skills that help them
                   Superior Critical                                                                      in a dungeon environment, such as climbing, finding and
                   Starting at 15th level, your weapon attacks score a criti-                             disarming traps, and opening locks.
                   cal hit on a roll of 18–20.                                                              When it comes to combat, rogues prioritize cunning
                                                                                                          over brute strength. A rogue would rather make one
                   Survivor                                                                               precise strike, placing it exactly where the attack will
                   At 18th level, you attain the pinnacle of resilience in bat-                           hurt the target most, than wear an opponent down with a
                   tle. At the start of each of your turns, you regain hit points                         barrage of attacks. Rogues have an almost supernatural
                   equal to 5 + your Constitution modifier if you have no                                 knack for avoiding danger, and a few learn magical tricks
                   more than half of your hit points left. You don’t gain this                            to supplement their other abilities.
                   benefit if you have 0 hit points.
                                                                                                          A Shady Living
                                                                                                          Every town and city has its share of rogues. Most of them
                                                                                                          live up to the worst stereotypes of the class, making a
                                                                                                          living as burglars, assassins, cutpurses, and con art-
                                                                                                          ists. Often, these scoundrels are organized into thieves’
                                                                                                          guilds or crime families. Plenty of rogues operate inde-
                                                                                                          pendently, but even they sometimes recruit apprentices to
                                                                                                          help them in their scams and heists. A few rogues make
                                                                                                          an honest living as locksmiths, investigators, or extermi-
                                                                                                          nators, which can be a dangerous job in a world where
                                                                                                          dire rats—and wererats—haunt the sewers.
                                                                                                             As adventurers, rogues fall on both sides of the law.
                                                                                                          Some are hardened criminals who decide to seek their
                                                                                                          fortune in treasure hoards, while others take up a life of
                                                                                                          adventure to escape from the law. Some have learned
                                                                                                          and perfected their skills with the explicit purpose of
                                                                                                          infiltrating ancient ruins and hidden crypts in search
                                                                                                          of treasure.
RICHARD WHITTERS




                                                                                                          Creating a Rogue
                                                                                                          As you create your rogue character, consider the char-
                                                                                                          acter’s relationship to the law. Do you have a criminal

At 3rd level, you choose an archetype that you emulate in

your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability

                                                                                      You hone your skills in the larcenous arts. Burglars,
Uncanny Dodge                                                                         bandits, cutpurses, and other criminals typically follow
Starting at 5th level, when an attacker that you can see                              this archetype, but so do rogues who prefer to think of
hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve                           themselves as professional treasure seekers, explorers,
the attack’s damage against you.                                                      delvers, and investigators. In addition to improving your
                                                                                      agility and stealth, you learn skills useful for delving into
Evasion                                                                               ancient ruins, reading unfamiliar languages, and using
                                                                                      magic items you normally couldn’t employ.
Beginning at 7th level, you can nimbly dodge out of the
way of certain area effects, such as a red dragon’s fiery                             Fast Hands
breath or an ice storm spell. When you are subjected                                  Starting at 3rd level, you can use the bonus action

damage if you fail.

By 15th level, you have acquired greater mental strength.                             By 13th level, you have learned enough about the work-
You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws.                                         ings of magic that you can improvise the use of items

Elusive                                                                               all class, race, and level requirements on the use of
                                                                                      magic items.
Beginning at 18th level, you are so evasive that attackers
rarely gain the upper hand against you. No attack roll has                            Thief’s Reflexes
advantage against you while you aren’t incapacitated.                                 When you reach 17th level, you have become adept at lay-
                                                                                      ing ambushes and quickly escaping danger. You can take
                                                                                      two turns during the first round of any combat. You take
                                                                                      your first turn at your normal initiative and your second
                                                                                      turn at your initiative minus 10. You can’t use this feature
                                                                                      when you are surprised.

Wizard                                                                                     Scholars of the Arcane
Clad in the silver robes that denote her station, an elf                                   Wild and enigmatic, varied in form and function, the
closes her eyes to shut out the distractions of the battle-                                power of magic draws students who seek to master its
field and begins her quiet chant. Fingers weaving in front                                 mysteries. Some aspire to become like the gods, shap-
of her, she completes her spell and launches a tiny bead                                   ing reality itself. Though the casting of a typical spell
of fire toward the enemy ranks, where it erupts into a con-                                requires merely the utterance of a few strange words,
flagration that engulfs the soldiers.                                                      fleeting gestures, and sometimes a pinch or clump of
   Checking and rechecking his work, a human scribes                                       exotic materials, these surface components barely hint at
an intricate magic circle in chalk on the bare stone                                       the expertise attained after years of apprenticeship and
floor, then sprinkles powdered iron along every line and                                   countless hours of study.
graceful curve. When the circle is complete, he drones                                        Wizards live and die by their spells. Everything else is
a long incantation. A hole opens in space inside the cir-                                  secondary. They learn new spells as they experiment and
cle, bringing a whiff of brimstone from the otherworldly                                   grow in experience. They can also learn them from other
plane beyond.                                                                              wizards, from ancient tomes or inscriptions, and from an-
   Crouching on the floor in a dungeon intersection, a                                     cient creatures (such as the fey) that are steeped in magic.
gnome tosses a handful of small bones inscribed with
mystic symbols, muttering a few words of power over                                        The Lure of Knowledge
them. Closing his eyes to see the visions more clearly,                                    Wizards’ lives are seldom mundane. The closest a wizard
he nods slowly, then opens his eyes and points down the                                    is likely to come to an ordinary life is working as a sage
passage to his left.                                                                       or lecturer in a library or university, teaching others the
   Wizards are supreme magic-users, defined and united                                     secrets of the multiverse. Other wizards sell their ser-
as a class by the spells they cast. Drawing on the subtle                                  vices as diviners, serve in military forces, or pursue lives
weave of magic that permeates the cosmos, wizards cast                                     of crime or domination.
spells of explosive fire, arcing lightning, subtle deception,                                 But the lure of knowledge and power calls even the
and brute-force mind control. Their magic conjures mon-                                    most unadventurous wizards out of the safety of their
sters from other planes of existence, glimpses the future,                                 libraries and laboratories and into crumbling ruins and
or turns slain foes into zombies. Their mightiest spells                                   lost cities. Most wizards believe that their counterparts
change one substance into another, call meteors down                                       in ancient civilizations knew secrets of magic that have
from the sky, or open portals to other worlds.                                             been lost to the ages, and discovering those secrets could
                                                                                           unlock the path to a power greater than any magic avail-
                                                                                           able in the present age.

                   School of Evocation                                                                   Overchannel
                   You focus your study on magic that creates powerful ele-                              Starting at 14th level, you can increase the power of
                   mental effects such as bitter cold, searing flame, rolling                            your simpler spells. When you cast a wizard spell of 1st
                   thunder, crackling lightning, and burning acid. Some                                  through 5th level that deals damage, you can deal maxi-
                   evokers find employment in military forces, serving as                                mum damage with that spell.
                   artillery to blast enemy armies from afar. Others use                                   The first time you do so, you suffer no adverse effect. If
                   their spectacular power to protect the weak, while some                               you use this feature again before you finish a long rest,
                   seek their own gain as bandits, adventurers, or aspir-                                you take 2d12 necrotic damage for each level of the spell,
                   ing tyrants.                                                                          immediately after you cast it. Each time you use this
                                                                                                         feature again before finishing a long rest, the necrotic
                   Evocation Savant                                                                      damage per spell level increases by 1d12. This damage
                   Beginning when you select this school at 2nd level, the                               ignores resistance and immunity.
                   gold and time you must spend to copy an evocation spell
                   into your spellbook is halved.

                   Sculpt Spells
                   Beginning at 2nd level, you can create pockets of relative
                   safety within the effects of your evocation spells. When
                   you cast an evocation spell that affects other creatures
                   that you can see, you can choose a number of them equal
                   to 1 + the spell’s level. The chosen creatures automati-
                   cally succeed on their saving throws against the spell,
                   and they take no damage if they would normally take half
                   damage on a successful save.

                   Potent Cantrip
                   Starting at 6th level, your damaging cantrips affect even
                   creatures that avoid the brunt of the effect. When a crea-
                   ture succeeds on a saving throw against your cantrip,
                   the creature takes half the cantrip’s damage (if any) but
                   suffers no additional effect from the cantrip.

                   Empowered Evocation
                   Beginning at 10th level, you can add your Intelligence
                   modifier to one damage roll of any wizard evocation
                   spell you cast.
RICHARD WHITTERS

Characters are defined by much more than their                                             You don’t need to be confined to binary notions of sex
race and class. They’re individuals with their own stories,                             and gender. The elf god Corellon Larethian is often seen
interests, connections, and capabilities beyond those                                   as androgynous, for example, and some elves in the mul-
that class and race define. This chapter expounds on the                                tiverse are made in Corellon’s image. You could also play
details that distinguish characters from one another, in-                               a female character who presents herself as a man, a man
cluding the basics of name and physical description, the                                who feels trapped in a female body, or a bearded female
rules of backgrounds and languages, and the finer points                                dwarf who hates being mistaken for a male. Likewise,
of personality and alignment.                                                           your character’s sexual orientation is for you to decide.

istics reflect the character you have in mind.                                          your character’s ability scores might say about his or
                                                                                        her height and weight. A weak but agile character might
Name                                                                                    be thin. A strong and tough character might be tall or

for members of that race. Put some thought into your

name even if you’re just picking one from a list.

Sex                                                                                     inches) beyond the base height. That same number mul-

any special benefits or hindrances. Think about how your                                Modifier column determines the character’s extra weight
character does or does not conform to the broader cul-                                  (in pounds) beyond the base weight.
ture’s expectations of sex, gender, and sexual behavior.

the surface.

  The details in this chapter make a big difference in setting
  your character apart from every other character. Consider                               Elf, high                 4'6"         +2d10          90 lb.    × (1d4) lb.
  the following two human fighters.                                                       Elf, wood                 4'6"         +2d10          100 lb.   × (1d4) lb.
     Hailing from the Dragonlance setting, Tika Waylan was a                              Halfling                  2'7"         +2d4           35 lb.    × 1 lb.
  brash teenager who had a rough childhood. The daughter of
  a thief, she ran away from home and practiced her father’s

     Artemis Entreri grew up on the streets of Calimport in the
  Forgotten Realms. He used his wits, strength, and agility                             You choose your character’s age and the color of his or
  to carve out his own territory in one of the city’s hundreds                          her hair, eyes, and skin. To add a touch of distinctiveness,
  of poor shanty towns. After several years, he attracted the                           you might want to give your character an unusual or
  notice of one of the most powerful thieves’ guilds in the                             memorable physical characteristic, such as a scar, a limp,
  city, and he ascended the ranks of the guild quickly despite                          or a tattoo.
  his youth. Artemis became the favored assassin of one
  of the city’s pashas, who sent him to far-off Icewind Dale                            Alignment
  to recover some stolen gems. He’s a professional killer,
  constantly challenging himself to improve his skills.                                 A typical creature in the worlds of Dungeons & Drag-
     Tika and Artemis are both human and both fighters (with                            ons has an alignment, which broadly describes its moral
  some experience as rogues), possessing similarly high                                 and personal attitudes. Alignment is a combination of
  Strength and Dexterity scores, but there the similarity ends.                         two factors: one identifies morality (good, evil, or neutral),
                                                                                        and the other describes attitudes toward society and

order (lawful, chaotic, or neutral). Thus, nine distinct                                    Tika and Artemis: Alignment
alignments define the possible combinations.                                                Tika Waylan is neutral good, fundamentally good-hearted
  These brief summaries of the nine alignments describe                                     and striving to help others where she can. Artemis is lawful
the typical behavior of a creature with that alignment.                                     evil, unconcerned with the value of sentient life but at least
Individuals might vary significantly from that typical                                      professional in his approach to murder.
behavior, and few people are perfectly and consistently                                       As an evil character, Artemis is not an ideal adventurer.
faithful to the precepts of their alignment.                                                He began his career as a villain, and only cooperates with
                                                                                            heroes when he must—and when it’s in his own best
  Lawful good (LG) creatures can be counted on to do
                                                                                            interests. In most games, evil adventurers cause problems
the right thing as expected by society. Gold dragons, pala-                                 in groups alongside others who don’t share their interests
dins, and most dwarves are lawful good.                                                     and objectives. Generally, evil alignments are for villains
  Neutral good (NG) folk do the best they can to help oth-                                  and monsters.
ers according to their needs. Many celestials, some cloud
giants, and most gnomes are neutral good.
                                                                                        Such a creature is incapable of making a moral or ethical
  Chaotic good (CG) creatures act as their conscience
                                                                                        choice and acts according to its bestial nature. Sharks
directs, with little regard for what others expect. Copper
                                                                                        are savage predators, for example, but they are not evil;
dragons, many elves, and unicorns are chaotic good.
                                                                                        they have no alignment.
  Lawful neutral (LN) individuals act in accordance with
law, tradition, or personal codes. Many monks and some
wizards are lawful neutral.
                                                                                        Languages
  Neutral (N) is the alignment of those who prefer to                                   Your race indicates the languages your character can
steer clear of moral questions and don’t take sides, doing                              speak by default, and your background might give you ac-
what seems best at the time. Lizardfolk, most druids, and                               cess to one or more additional languages of your choice.
many humans are neutral.                                                                Note these languages on your character sheet.

holding their personal freedom above all else. Many bar-                                table, or choose one that is common in your campaign.
barians and rogues, and some bards, are chaotic neutral.                                With your DM’s permission, you can instead choose a

want, within the limits of a code of tradition, loyalty, or or-                         guage, such as thieves’ cant or the tongue of druids.
der. Devils, blue dragons, and hobgoblins are lawful evil.                                Some of these languages are actually families of lan-
  Neutral evil (NE) is the alignment of those who do                                    guages with many dialects. For example, the Primordial
whatever they can get away with, without compassion or                                  language includes the Auran, Aquan, Ignan, and Terran

are neutral evil.                                                                       tures that speak different dialects of the same language

spurred by their greed, hatred, or bloodlust. Demons, red

                                                                                          Language                    Typical Speakers              Script
Alignment in the Multiverse
                                                                                          Common                      Humans                        Common
For many thinking creatures, alignment is a moral
                                                                                          Dwarvish                    Dwarves                       Dwarvish
choice. Humans, dwarves, elves, and other humanoid
races can choose whether to follow the paths of good or                                   Elvish                      Elves                         Elvish
evil, law or chaos. According to myth, the good-aligned                                   Giant                       Ogres, giants                 Dwarvish
gods who created these races gave them free will to                                       Gnomish                     Gnomes                        Dwarvish
choose their moral paths, knowing that good without free                                  Goblin                      Goblinoids                    Dwarvish
will is slavery.                                                                          Halfling                    Halflings                     Common
   The evil deities who created other races, though, made                                 Orc                         Orcs                          Dwarvish
those races to serve them. Those races have strong
inborn tendencies that match the nature of their gods.
                                                                                        Exotic Languages
Most orcs share the violent, savage nature of the orc god,
Gruumsh, and are thus inclined toward evil. Even if an                                    Language                    Typical Speakers              Script
orc chooses a good alignment, it struggles against its in-                                Abyssal                     Demons                        Infernal
nate tendencies for its entire life. (Even half-orcs feel the                             Celestial                   Celestials                    Celestial
lingering pull of the orc god’s influence.)                                               Deep Speech                 Mind flayers,                 —
   Alignment is an essential part of the nature of celes-                                                             beholders
tials and fiends. A devil does not choose to be lawful evil,                              Draconic                    Dragons,                      Draconic
and it doesn’t tend toward lawful evil, but rather it is law-                                                         dragonborn
ful evil in its essence. If it somehow ceased to be lawful                                Infernal                    Devils                        Infernal
evil, it would cease to be a devil.
                                                                                          Primordial                  Elementals                    Dwarvish
   Most creatures that lack the capacity for rational
                                                                                          Sylvan                      Fey creatures                 Elvish
thought do not have alignments—they are unaligned.

ual gestures, vices and pet peeves, and whatever else you

Personality Traits                                                                     personality traits, a flaw might answer any of these ques-
Give your character two personality traits. Personality                                tions: What enrages you? What’s the one person, concept,
traits are small, simple ways to help you set your char-                               or event that you are terrified of? What are your vices?
acter apart from every other character. Your personality

your character. They should be self-descriptions that are                              Inspiration
specific about what makes your character stand out. “I’m                               Inspiration is a rule the Dungeon Master can use to re-
smart” is not a good trait, because it describes a lot of                              ward you for playing your character in a way that’s true
characters. “I’ve read every book in Candlekeep” tells you                             to his or her personality traits, ideal, bond, and flaw. By
something specific about your character’s interests and                                using inspiration, you can draw on your personality trait
disposition.                                                                           of compassion for the downtrodden to give you an edge in

character dislikes or fears, your character’s self-attitude                            to push past the effect of a spell that has been laid on you.
or mannerisms, or the influence of his or her abil-
ity scores.                                                                            Gaining Inspiration
   A useful place to start thinking about personality traits                           Your DM can choose to give you inspiration for a variety
is to look at your highest and lowest ability scores and de-                           of reasons. Typically, DMs award it when you play out
fine one trait related to each. Either one could be positive                           your personality traits, give in to the drawbacks pre-
or negative: you might work hard to overcome a low score,                              sented by a flaw or bond, and otherwise portray your
for example, or be cocky about your high score.                                        character in a compelling way. Your DM will tell you how
                                                                                       you can earn inspiration in the game.
Ideals                                                                                   You either have inspiration or you don’t—you can’t

are the things that you believe in most strongly, the fun-

the principles that you will never betray? What would                                  your inspiration gives you advantage on that roll.
prompt you to make sacrifices? What drives you to act
and guides your goals and ambitions? What is the single                                    Tika and Artemis: Character Details

Folk Hero                                                                                   d8     Personality Trait
You come from a humble social rank, but you are                                              1     I judge people by their actions, not their words.
destined for so much more. Already the people of                                             2     If someone is in trouble, I’m always ready to lend help.
your home village regard you as their champion, and                                          3     When I set my mind to something, I follow through no
your destiny calls you to stand against the tyrants and                                            matter what gets in my way.
monsters that threaten the common folk everywhere.                                           4     I have a strong sense of fair play and always try to find
                                                                                                   the most equitable solution to arguments.
Skill Proficiencies: Animal Handling, Survival
                                                                                             5     I’m confident in my own abilities and do what I can to
Tool Proficiencies: One type of artisan’s tools,
 vehicles (land)                                                                                   instill confidence in others.

 a shovel, an iron pot, a set of common clothes, and a                                       7     I misuse long words in an attempt to sound smarter.
 pouch containing 10 gp                                                                      8     I get bored easily. When am I going to get on
                                                                                                   with my destiny?
Defining Event
You previously pursued a simple profession among the                                        d6     Ideal
peasantry, perhaps as a farmer, miner, servant, shepherd,
                                                                                             1     Respect. People deserve to be treated with dignity and
woodcutter, or gravedigger. But something happened that
                                                                                                   respect. (Good)
set you on a different path and marked you for greater
things. Choose or randomly determine a defining event                                        2     Fairness. No one should get preferential treatment
that marked you as a hero of the people.                                                           before the law, and no one is above the law. (Lawful)
                                                                                             3     Freedom. Tyrants must not be allowed to oppress the
 d10   Defining Event                                                                              people. (Chaotic)
  1    I stood up to a tyrant’s agents.                                                      4     Might. If I become strong, I can take what I want—what
  2    I saved people during a natural disaster.                                                   I deserve. (Evil)
  3    I stood alone against a terrible monster.                                             5     Sincerity. There’s no good in pretending to be

  5    I led a militia to fight off an invading army.                                        6     Destiny. Nothing and no one can steer me away from
  6    I broke into a tyrant’s castle and stole weapons to                                         my higher calling. (Any)
       arm the people.
  7    I trained the peasantry to use farm implements as                                    d6     Bond
       weapons against a tyrant’s soldiers.                                                  1     I have a family, but I have no idea where they are. One
  8    A lord rescinded an unpopular decree after I led a                                          day, I hope to see them again.
       symbolic act of protest against it.                                                   2     I worked the land, I love the land, and I will

       revealed my secret origin.                                                            3     A proud noble once gave me a horrible beating, and I

       was commended for my heroism.                                                         4     My tools are symbols of my past life, and I carry them
                                                                                                   so that I will never forget my roots.

fit in among them with ease. You can find a place to hide,                                         pursue my destiny.
rest, or recuperate among other commoners, unless you
have shown yourself to be a danger to them. They will

shield you from the law or anyone else searching for you,
                                                                                             1     The tyrant who rules my land will stop at nothing to
though they will not risk their lives for you.
                                                                                                   see me killed.

A folk hero is one of the common people, for better or for                                         blind to my shortcomings and the risk of failure.
worse. Most folk heroes look on their humble origins as                                      3     The people who knew me when I was young know my
a virtue, not a shortcoming, and their home communities                                            shameful secret, so I can never go home again.

Noble                                                                                      d8     Personality Trait
You understand wealth, power, and privilege. You carry                                      1     My eloquent flattery makes everyone I talk to feel like
a noble title, and your family owns land, collects taxes,                                         the most wonderful and important person in the world.
and wields significant political influence. You might be                                    2     The common folk love me for my kindness
a pampered aristocrat unfamiliar with work or discom-                                             and generosity.
fort, a former merchant just elevated to the nobility, or                                   3     No one could doubt by looking at my regal bearing that I
a disinherited scoundrel with a disproportionate sense                                            am a cut above the unwashed masses.
of entitlement. Or you could be an honest, hard-working                                     4     I take great pains to always look my best and follow the
landowner who cares deeply about the people who live
                                                                                                  latest fashions.
and work on your land, keenly aware of your responsibil-
                                                                                            5     I don’t like to get my hands dirty, and I won’t be caught
ity to them.
   Work with your DM to come up with an appropriate ti-                                           dead in unsuitable accommodations.
tle and determine how much authority that title carries. A                                  6     Despite my noble birth, I do not place myself above
noble title doesn’t stand on its own—it’s connected to an                                         other folk. We all have the same blood.
entire family, and whatever title you hold, you will pass it                                7     My favor, once lost, is lost forever.
down to your own children. Not only do you need to deter-                                   8     If you do me an injury, I will crush you, ruin your name,
mine your noble title, but you should also work with the                                          and salt your fields.
DM to describe your family and their influence on you.

recently bestowed? How much influence do they wield,

family have among the other aristocrats of the region?
                                                                                                  with dignity. (Good)

tle? How do you feel about that responsibility? Or are you                                        mine. (Lawful)
so far down the line of inheritance that no one cares what                                  3     Independence. I must prove that I can handle myself
you do, as long as you don’t embarrass the family? How                                            without the coddling of my family. (Chaotic)
does the head of your family feel about your adventuring                                    4     Power. If I can attain more power, no one will tell me
career? Are you in your family’s good graces, or shunned                                          what to do. (Evil)
by the rest of your family?                                                                 5     Family. Blood runs thicker than water. (Any)

might wear on a signet ring? Particular colors you wear

all the time? An animal you regard as a symbol of your

as features of the world of the campaign.                                                   1     I will face any challenge to win the approval of my family.

Languages: One of your choice                                                               3     Nothing is more important than the other members

 pedigree, and a purse containing 25 gp                                                     4     I am in love with the heir of a family that my
                                                                                                  family despises.

Thanks to your noble birth, people are inclined to think                                    6     The common folk must see me as a hero of the people.
the best of you. You are welcome in high society, and
people assume you have the right to be wherever you are.
                                                                                           d6     Flaw
The common folk make every effort to accommodate you
                                                                                            1     I secretly believe that everyone is beneath me.
and avoid your displeasure, and other people of high birth
treat you as a member of the same social sphere. You can                                    2     I hide a truly scandalous secret that could ruin my
secure an audience with a local noble if you need to.                                             family forever.
                                                                                            3     I too often hear veiled insults and threats in every word
Suggested Characteristics                                                                         addressed to me, and I’m quick to anger.
Nobles are born and raised to a very different lifestyle                                    4     I have an insatiable desire for carnal pleasures.
than most people ever experience, and their personalities                                   5     In fact, the world does revolve around me.
reflect that upbringing. A noble title comes with a pleth-                                  6     By my words and actions, I often bring shame
ora of bonds—responsibilities to family, to other nobles
                                                                                                  to my family.
(including the sovereign), to the people entrusted to the
family’s care, or even to the title itself. But this responsi-
bility is often a good way to undermine a noble.

The marketplace of a large city teems with buyers                                         With one gold piece, a character can buy a bedroll, 50
and sellers of many sorts: dwarf smiths and elf woodcarv-                              feet of good rope, or a goat. A skilled (but not exceptional)
ers, halfling farmers and gnome jewelers, not to mention                               artisan can earn one gold piece a day. The gold piece is
humans of every shape, size, and color drawn from a                                    the standard unit of measure for wealth, even if the coin
spectrum of nations and cultures. In the largest cities,                               itself is not commonly used. When merchants discuss
almost anything imaginable is offered for sale, from ex-                               deals that involve goods or services worth hundreds or
otic spices and luxurious clothing to wicker baskets and                               thousands of gold pieces, the transactions don’t usually
practical swords.                                                                      involve the exchange of individual coins. Rather, the gold
  For an adventurer, the availability of armor, weapons,                               piece is a standard measure of value, and the actual ex-
backpacks, rope, and similar goods is of paramount                                     change is in gold bars, letters of credit, or valuable goods.
importance, since proper equipment can mean the differ-                                   One gold piece is worth ten silver pieces, the most
ence between life and death in a dungeon or the untamed                                prevalent coin among commoners. A silver piece buys
wilds. This chapter details the mundane and exotic mer-                                a laborer’s work for half a day, a flask of lamp oil, or a
chandise that adventurers commonly find useful in the face                             night’s rest in a poor inn.
of the threats that the worlds of D&D present.                                            One silver piece is worth ten copper pieces, which are
                                                                                       common among laborers and beggars. A single copper
Starting Equipment                                                                     piece buys a candle, a torch, or a piece of chalk.

ble to determine how much gold you have to spend.

                                                                                       you return to a town or other settlement, provided that you
Wealth                                                                                 can find buyers and merchants interested in your loot.
                                                                                          Arms, Armor, and Other Equipment. As a general
Wealth appears in many forms in a D&D world. Coins,                                    rule, undamaged weapons, armor, and other equipment
gemstones, trade goods, art objects, animals, and prop-                                fetch half their cost when sold in a market. Weapons and
erty can reflect your character’s financial well-being.                                armor used by monsters are rarely in good enough condi-
Members of the peasantry trade in goods, bartering for                                 tion to sell.
what they need and paying taxes in grain and cheese.                                      Magic Items. Selling magic items is problematic. Find-
Members of the nobility trade either in legal rights, such                             ing someone to buy a potion or a scroll isn’t too hard, but
as the rights to a mine, a port, or farmland, or in gold                               other items are out of the realm of most but the wealthiest
bars, measuring gold by the pound rather than by the                                   nobles. Likewise, aside from a few common magic items,
coin. Only merchants, adventurers, and those offering pro-                             you won’t normally come across magic items or spells to
fessional services for hire commonly deal in coins.                                    purchase. The value of magic is far beyond simple gold
                                                                                       and should always be treated as such.

based on the relative worth of the metal from which they                               trade them in for coin or use them as currency for other
are made. The three most common coins are the gold                                     transactions. For exceptionally valuable treasures, the
piece (gp), the silver piece (sp), and the copper piece (cp).

duct transactions through barter. Like gems and art                                         common sense. For example, a burly half-orc won’t fit in a
objects, trade goods—bars of iron, bags of salt, livestock,                                 halfling’s leather armor, and a gnome would be swallowed
and so on—retain their full value in the market and can                                     up in a cloud giant’s elegant robe.
be used as currency.                                                                          The DM can impose more realism. For example, a suit

Armor and Shields                                                                           one without significant alterations, and a guard’s uniform
                                                                                            might be visibly ill-fitting when an adventurer tries to wear it
D&D worlds are a vast tapestry made up of many dif-                                         as a disguise.
ferent cultures, each with its own technology level. For                                      Using this variant, when adventurers find armor, clothing,
                                                                                            and similar items that are made to be worn, they might
this reason, adventurers have access to a variety of ar-
                                                                                            need to visit an armorsmith, tailor, leatherworker, or similar
mor types, ranging from leather armor to chain mail to                                      expert to make the item wearable. The cost for such work
costly plate armor, with several other kinds of armor in                                    varies from 10 to 40 percent of the market price of the item.
between. The Armor table collects the most commonly                                         The DM can either roll 1d4 × 10 or determine the increase
available types of armor found in the game and separates                                    in cost based on the extent of the alterations required.
them into three categories: light armor, medium armor,
and heavy armor. Many warriors supplement their armor                                     Stealth. If the Armor table shows “Disadvantage” in
with a shield.                                                                          the Stealth column, the wearer has disadvantage on Dex-
  The Armor table shows the cost, weight, and other                                     terity (Stealth) checks.
properties of the common types of armor worn in the                                       Shields. A shield is made from wood or metal and is
worlds of D&D.                                                                          carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your

or strap a shield to an arm. Only those proficient in the                               at a time.
armor’s use know how to wear it effectively, however.

armor. If you wear armor that you lack proficiency with,
you have disadvantage on any ability check, saving throw,                               Made from supple and thin materials, light armor favors
or attack roll that involves Strength or Dexterity, and you                             agile adventurers since it offers some protection without
can’t cast spells.                                                                      sacrificing mobility. If you wear light armor, you add your

tacks. The armor (and shield) you wear determines your                                  type to determine your Armor Class.
base Armor Class.                                                                         Padded. Padded armor consists of quilted layers of
  Heavy Armor. Heavier armor interferes with the wear-                                  cloth and batting.
er’s ability to move quickly, stealthily, and freely. If the                              Leather. The breastplate and shoulder protectors of

Medium Armor                                                                            Donning and Doffing Armor
Medium armor offers more protection than light armor,                                     Category                        Don                       Doff
but it also impairs movement more. If you wear medium                                     Light Armor                     1 minute                  1 minute
armor, you add your Dexterity modifier, to a maximum                                      Medium Armor                    5 minutes                 1 minute
of +2, to the base number from your armor type to deter-                                  Heavy Armor                     10 minutes                5 minutes
mine your Armor Class.                                                                    Shield                          1 action                  1 action
   Hide. This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts.
It is commonly worn by barbarian tribes, evil humanoids,
and other folk who lack access to the tools and materials                               Weapons
needed to create better armor.                                                          Your class grants proficiency in certain weapons, reflect-
   Chain Shirt. Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain                               ing both the class’s focus and the tools you are most likely
shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This                               to use. Whether you favor a longsword or a longbow, your
armor offers modest protection to the wearer’s upper                                    weapon and your ability to wield it effectively can mean
body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against                                  the difference between life and death while adventuring.
one another to be muffled by outer layers.                                                The Weapons table shows the most common weapons
   Scale Mail. This armor consists of a coat and leggings                               used in the worlds of D&D, their price and weight, the
(and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with                                  damage they deal when they hit, and any special prop-
overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a                                  erties they possess. Every weapon is classified as either
fish. The suit includes gauntlets.                                                      melee or ranged. A melee weapon is used to attack a
   Breastplate. This armor consists of a fitted metal chest                             target within 5 feet of you, whereas a ranged weapon is
piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the                                  used to attack a target at a distance.
legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides
good protection for the wearer’s vital organs while leav-                               Weapon Proficiency
ing the wearer relatively unencumbered.                                                 Your race, class, and feats can grant you proficiency
   Half Plate. Half plate consists of shaped metal plates                               with certain weapons or categories of weapons. The two
that cover most of the wearer’s body. It does not include                               categories are simple and martial. Most people can
leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached                                  use simple weapons with proficiency. These weapons
with leather straps.                                                                    include clubs, maces, and other weapons often found in
                                                                                        the hands of commoners. Martial weapons, including
Heavy Armor                                                                             swords, axes, and polearms, require more specialized
Of all the armor categories, heavy armor offers the best                                training to use effectively. Most warriors use martial
protection. These suits of armor cover the entire body                                  weapons because these weapons put their fighting style
and are designed to stop a wide range of attacks. Only                                  and training to best use.
proficient warriors can manage their weight and bulk.                                      Proficiency with a weapon allows you to add your pro-
   Heavy armor doesn’t let you add your Dexterity modi-                                 ficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make
fier to your Armor Class, but it also doesn’t penalize you                              with that weapon. If you make an attack roll using a
if your Dexterity modifier is negative.                                                 weapon with which you lack proficiency, you do not add
   Ring Mail. This armor is leather armor with heavy                                    your proficiency bonus to the attack roll.
rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor
against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior                               Weapon Properties
to chain mail, and it’s usually worn only by those who                                  Many weapons have special properties related to their use,
can’t afford better armor.                                                              as shown in the Weapons table.
   Chain Mail. Made of interlocking metal rings, chain                                     Ammunition. You can use a weapon that has the am-
mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath                                 munition property to make a ranged attack only if you
the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of                                have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you
blows. The suit includes gauntlets.                                                     attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammu-
   Splint. This armor is made of narrow vertical strips                                 nition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or
of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over                              other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand
cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.                                 to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle,
   Plate. Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal                                  you can recover half your expended ammunition by tak-
plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes                               ing a minute to search the battlefield.
gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and                                      If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to
thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles                                   make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised
and straps distribute the weight over the body.                                         weapon (see “Improvised Weapons” later in the section).

Getting Into and Out of Armor                                                           in this way.

is shown in the Donning and Doffing Armor table.                                        weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexter-

   Doff. This is the time it takes to take off the item. If you

                                                                                         Expenses
                                                                                         When not descending into the depths of the earth, explor-
Chariot                                      250 gp              100 lb.
                                                                                         ing ruins for lost treasures, or waging war against the
Feed (per day)                                 5 cp               10 lb.
                                                                                         encroaching darkness, adventurers face more mundane
Saddle                                                                                   realities. Even in a fantastical world, people require basic
   Exotic                                      60 gp              40 lb.                 necessities such as shelter, sustenance, and clothing.
   Military                                    20 gp              30 lb.                 These things cost money, although some lifestyles cost
   Pack                                         5 gp              15 lb.                 more than others.
   Riding                                      10 gp              25 lb.
Saddlebags                                      4 gp               8 lb.                 Lifestyle Expenses
Sled                                           20 gp             300 lb.                 Lifestyle expenses provide you with a simple way to ac-
Stabling (per day)                              5 sp                 —                   count for the cost of living in a fantasy world. They cover
Wagon                                          35 gp             400 lb.                 your accommodations, food and drink, and all your other
                                                                                         necessities. Furthermore, expenses cover the cost of
Waterborne Vehicles                                                                      maintaining your equipment so you can be ready when
                                                                                         adventure next calls.
Item                                          Cost               Speed
                                                                                            At the start of each week or month (your choice),
Galley                                   30,000 gp              4 mph                    choose a lifestyle from the Expenses table and pay the
Keelboat                                  3,000 gp              1 mph                    price to sustain that lifestyle. The prices listed are per
Longship                                 10,000 gp              3 mph                    day, so if you wish to calculate the cost of your chosen
Rowboat                                      50 gp             1½ mph                    lifestyle over a thirty-day period, multiply the listed price
Sailing ship                             10,000 gp              2 mph                    by 30. Your lifestyle might change from one period to the
Warship                                  25,000 gp             2½ mph                    next, based on the funds you have at your disposal, or you
                                                                                         might maintain the same lifestyle throughout your char-
                                                                                         acter’s career.
Trade Goods                                                                                 Your lifestyle choice can have consequences. Main-
Most wealth is not in coins. It is measured in livestock,                                taining a wealthy lifestyle might help you make contacts
grain, land, rights to collect taxes, or rights to resources                             with the rich and powerful, though you run the risk of at-
(such as a mine or a forest).                                                            tracting thieves. Likewise, living frugally might help you
  Guilds, nobles, and royalty regulate trade. Chartered                                  avoid criminals, but you are unlikely to make powerful
companies are granted rights to conduct trade along cer-                                 connections.
tain routes, to send merchant ships to various ports, or to
buy or sell specific goods. Guilds set prices for the goods                              Lifestyle Expenses
or services that they control, and determine who may                                       Lifestyle                                    Price/Day
or may not offer those goods and services. Merchants                                       Wretched                                             —
commonly exchange trade goods without using currency.                                      Squalid                                            1 sp
The Trade Goods table shows the value of commonly ex-                                      Poor                                               2 sp
changed goods.                                                                             Modest                                             1 gp
                                                                                           Comfortable                                        2 gp
                                                                                           Wealthy                                            4 gp
                                                                                           Aristocratic                           10 gp minimum
